rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Additional Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 27, 2021 line 1. PETITIONER 345 CALIFORNIA L.P.'S Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Additional Writ Of Attachment is GRANTED in the amount of $364.573.20 in past due rent for May 2021, plus $240,000 in incurred and estimated fees to resolve breach of lease arbitration; $81,840 in costs for JAMS fees and expert costs. =(501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Umpqua Banks Notice Of Motion And Motion For Payment Of Its Attorneys Fees And Costs From Cash On Hand In The Receivership Estate In Connection With Receivers Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Re: Approving And Settling Receivers Final Report And Accounting; Etc.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 27, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF UMPQUA BANK'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Payment Of Its Attorneys Fees And Costs From Cash On Hand In The Receivership Estate In Connection With Receivers Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Re: Approving And Settling Receivers Final Report And Accounting; Etc. is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for determination of entitlement to payment of fees out of receivership estate is granted. Motion for payment of fees is denied without prejudice. Moving party failed to address in the reply the argument raised in opposition that the motion for determination of attorney's fees is premature as no judgment in favor of the Plaintiff has yet been entered. The disputed amount of fees shall be deposited with the Court upon approval of the final accounting and distribution and Plaintiff may renew its motion it a timely fashion thereafter. =(501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Sue Receiver In Independent Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 27, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT XELAN PROP 1, LLC'S Notice Of Motion To Sue Receiver In Independent Action Hearing Required to address whether claims raised in the Defendant's Motion to Sue the Receiver shall be addressed before the approval of final report. Southern California Sunbelt Developers, Inc. v. Banyan Limited Partnership (2017) 8 Cal.App.5th 910 as opposed to a separate action after the approval. If a determination is made that the claims have to be addressed before the approval, this motion will be moot. To the extent the motion seeks to sue the receiver in a different venue, the motion is denied. =(501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
